0088_base_package_commands.Rmd

last updated 16 May 2021

PURPOSE: Tools to manage R packages. TODO: Study code:
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/4090169/elegant-way-to-check-for-missing-packages-and-install-them

SEE ALSO: ~/code/zsh_scripts_project/071_run_R_to_update_packages.sh

knitr::all_labels()
      ##  [1] "setup"              "knit_exit2"         "libPaths"          
      ##  [4] "list_user"          "repos"              "remove_package"    
      ##  [7] "installed.packages" "begin"              "now"               
      ## [10] "update_old"         "upgrade"            "installed_use_DT"  
      ## [13] "library"            "default"            "install"           
      ## [16] "update"             "trouble"            "dependencies"      
      ## [19] "package_info"       "startup"            "description"       
      ## [22] "knit_exit()"        "render"
# knitr::knit_exit()

.libPaths() to find user and common library locations

# in my home directory, and
# in /usr/local/lib/R/library
.libPaths()
      ## [1] "/home/jim/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.0"
      ## [2] "/usr/local/lib/R/library"

list packages in user library

# top level dir only (so not recursive)
pkgs  <- lapply(.libPaths(), list.dirs, recursive=FALSE)

# user - 288!
length(pkgs[[1]])
      ## [1] 323
# common - 30  
length(pkgs[[2]])
      ## [1] 30

What repos are packages from?

options("repos")
      ## $repos
      ##                          CRAN 
      ## "https://cloud.r-project.org"

remove a package

  if (F) {
    pkg = 
  remove.packages(pkg)
  }

Examine installed.packages

  • See below, use DT to ask Q
installed  <- as_tibble(installed.packages())

# 6 x 16
head(installed) 
      ## # A tibble: 6 x 16
      ##   Package  LibPath   Version Priority Depends Imports   LinkingTo Suggests
      ##   <chr>    <chr>     <chr>   <chr>    <chr>   <chr>     <chr>     <chr>   
      ## 1 abind    /home/ji… 1.4-5   <NA>     R (>= … "methods… <NA>      <NA>    
      ## 2 airports /home/ji… 0.1.0   <NA>     R (>= …  <NA>     <NA>      testthat
      ## 3 alabama  /home/ji… 2015.3… <NA>     R (>= …  <NA>     <NA>      <NA>    
      ## 4 ape      /home/ji… 5.5     <NA>     R (>= … "nlme, l… Rcpp      gee, ex…
      ## 5 AsioHea… /home/ji… 1.16.1… <NA>     <NA>     <NA>     <NA>      <NA>    
      ## 6 askpass  /home/ji… 1.1     <NA>     <NA>    "sys (>=… <NA>      testthat
      ## # … with 8 more variables: Enhances <chr>, License <chr>,
      ## #   License_is_FOSS <chr>, License_restricts_use <chr>, OS_type <chr>,
      ## #   MD5sum <chr>, NeedsCompilation <chr>, Built <chr>
str(installed)
      ## tibble[,16] [352 × 16] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
      ##  $ Package              : chr [1:352] "abind" "airports" "alabama" "ape" ...
      ##  $ LibPath              : chr [1:352] "/home/jim/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.0" "/home/jim/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.0" "/home/jim/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.0" "/home/jim/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.0" ...
      ##  $ Version              : chr [1:352] "1.4-5" "0.1.0" "2015.3-1" "5.5" ...
      ##  $ Priority             : chr [1:352] NA NA NA NA ...
      ##  $ Depends              : chr [1:352] "R (>= 1.5.0)" "R (>= 2.10)" "R (>= 2.10.1), numDeriv" "R (>= 3.2.0)" ...
      ##  $ Imports              : chr [1:352] "methods, utils" NA NA "nlme, lattice, graphics, methods, stats, tools, utils,\nparallel, Rcpp (>= 0.12.0)" ...
      ##  $ LinkingTo            : chr [1:352] NA NA NA "Rcpp" ...
      ##  $ Suggests             : chr [1:352] NA "testthat" NA "gee, expm, igraph, phangorn" ...
      ##  $ Enhances             : chr [1:352] NA NA NA NA ...
      ##  $ License              : chr [1:352] "LGPL (>= 2)" "GPL-3" "GPL (>= 2)" "GPL-2 | GPL-3" ...
      ##  $ License_is_FOSS      : chr [1:352] NA NA NA NA ...
      ##  $ License_restricts_use: chr [1:352] NA NA NA NA ...
      ##  $ OS_type              : chr [1:352] NA NA NA NA ...
      ##  $ MD5sum               : chr [1:352] NA NA NA NA ...
      ##  $ NeedsCompilation     : chr [1:352] "no" "no" "no" "yes" ...
      ##  $ Built                : chr [1:352] "4.0.2" "4.0.2" "4.0.4" "4.0.4" ...
# 317
installed[,"NeedsCompilation" == "no"]
      ## # A tibble: 352 x 0
installed[,"NeedsCompilation" == "yes"]
      ## # A tibble: 352 x 0

Examine available.packages() (>17k)

versions  <- available.packages()

t  <- as_tibble(versions)
t[,c(1,2,12:13,15:16)]
      ## # A tibble: 17,589 x 6
      ##    Package     Version OS_type Archs NeedsCompilation File 
      ##    <chr>       <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr>            <chr>
      ##  1 A3          1.0.0   <NA>    <NA>  no               <NA> 
      ##  2 aaSEA       1.1.0   <NA>    <NA>  no               <NA> 
      ##  3 AATtools    0.0.1   <NA>    <NA>  no               <NA> 
      ##  4 ABACUS      1.0.0   <NA>    <NA>  no               <NA> 
      ##  5 abbyyR      0.5.5   <NA>    <NA>  no               <NA> 
      ##  6 abc         2.1     <NA>    <NA>  no               <NA> 
      ##  7 abc.data    1.0     <NA>    <NA>  no               <NA> 
      ##  8 ABC.RAP     0.9.0   <NA>    <NA>  no               <NA> 
      ##  9 abcADM      1.0     <NA>    <NA>  yes              <NA> 
      ## 10 ABCanalysis 1.2.1   <NA>    <NA>  no               <NA> 
      ## # … with 17,579 more rows
str(versions)
      ##  chr [1:17589, 1:17] "A3" "aaSEA" "AATtools" "ABACUS" "abbyyR" "abc" ...
      ##  - attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2
      ##   ..$ : chr [1:17589] "A3" "aaSEA" "AATtools" "ABACUS" ...
      ##   ..$ : chr [1:17] "Package" "Version" "Priority" "Depends" ...
names(versions)
      ## NULL
typeof(versions)
      ## [1] "character"
length(versions)
      ## [1] 299013
versions[[4]]
      ## [1] "ABACUS"
head(versions)
      ##          Package    Version Priority
      ## A3       "A3"       "1.0.0" NA

Where are we now?

  • which dir are used? (for user pkgs, for common pkgs)
  • status of pkgs in each dir
# packages that can be upgraded
old.packages()
      ##        Package  LibPath                                       Installed
      ## pillar "pillar" "/home/jim/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.0" "1.6.0"  
      ## tibble "tibble" "/home/jim/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.0" "3.1.1"  
      ##        Built   ReposVer Repository                               
      ## pillar "4.0.4" "1.6.1"  "https://cloud.r-project.org/src/contrib"
      ## tibble "4.0.4" "3.1.2"  "https://cloud.r-project.org/src/contrib"
# x is list
x  <- packageStatus()
typeof(x)
      ## [1] "list"
str(x)
      ## List of 2
      ##  $ inst :'data.frame': 352 obs. of  17 variables:

  • May 16, 2021 - best code is in jimTools:: Rscripts ### ========================= ### UPDATE - 2 step process? ### jimTools - has newer version of this ### =========================

To update old packages

  • Appears that need to do this first, before update others
  • To increase old to include those comipiled earlier minor version of R
  • Use checkBuilt= T to force
update.packages(ask = F,
                oldPkgs = old.packages(),
                checkBuilt=F)

To upgrade

vs update.packages() ?

{
begin  <- Sys.time()
    update.packages(ask = FALSE , checkBuild=TRUE)
    end  <- Sys.time()
    diff  <- end - begin
    diff
}
      ## Time difference of 1.8 secs

NOTES: re: install on Rpi

I installed R 4.0.4 from source on Raspberry Pi 400, which uses ARM processessors. This seemed fine.

However, when I tried to upgrade installed packages I ran into a few problems.

My question is: After installing R 4.0.4 but before upgrading packages, what steps should I take to insure the package upgrade will be successful?

This is what I did do; which did not succeed:

First, I ran packageStatus to see ~29 packages needed an upgrade. >> x <- packageStatus() >> print(x) >>

Then a did the upgrade which reported ~ 31 warnings (packages that had non-zero exit status). About 1 or 2 packages did upgrade successfully.

upgrade(x, ask=FALSE) update(x)

However, I also noted errors during the upgrade such as: * Wrong ELF class ELFCLASS64. * lazy loading failed for package: “DT”

install.packages (using DT)

library(data.table)
      ## data.table 1.14.0 using 1 threads (see ?getDTthreads).  Latest news: r-datatable.com
      ## 
      ## Attaching package: 'data.table'
      ## The following objects are masked from 'package:dplyr':
      ## 
      ##     between, first, last
      ## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
      ## 
      ##     transpose
      ## The following object is masked from 'package:rlang':
      ## 
      ##     :=
library(microbenchmark)

# 4.5 ms
microbenchmark( installed  <- tibble::as_tibble(installed.packages()))
      ## Unit: milliseconds
      ##                                                  expr min  lq mean median
      ##  installed <- tibble::as_tibble(installed.packages()) 4.8 4.9  5.2    5.2
      ##   uq max neval
      ##  5.4 6.8   100
dt  <- as.data.table(installed)
names(dt)
      ##  [1] "Package"               "LibPath"              
      ##  [3] "Version"               "Priority"             
      ##  [5] "Depends"               "Imports"              
      ##  [7] "LinkingTo"             "Suggests"             
      ##  [9] "Enhances"              "License"              
      ## [11] "License_is_FOSS"       "License_restricts_use"
      ## [13] "OS_type"               "MD5sum"               
      ## [15] "NeedsCompilation"      "Built"
# remove uneeded fields
# .()  is alias for list()
dt  <- dt[, .(Package,  OS_type, NeedsCompilation, Built)]
dt
      ##          Package OS_type NeedsCompilation Built
      ##   1:       abind    <NA>               no 4.0.2
      ##   2:    airports    <NA>               no 4.0.2
      ##   3:     alabama    <NA>               no 4.0.4
      ##   4:         ape    <NA>              yes 4.0.4
      ##   5: AsioHeaders    <NA>               no 4.0.4
      ##  ---                                           
      ## 348:      stats4    <NA>             <NA> 4.0.4
      ## 349:    survival    <NA>              yes 4.0.4
      ## 350:       tcltk    <NA>              yes 4.0.4
      ## 351:       tools    <NA>              yes 4.0.4
      ## 352:       utils    <NA>              yes 4.0.4
# count, group by version of Build
dt[,.(.N)    , by=.(Built)]
      ##    Built   N
      ## 1: 4.0.2  66
      ## 2: 4.0.4 269
      ## 3: 4.0.3  17
# same
dt[, .N, by = .(Built)]   
      ##    Built   N
      ## 1: 4.0.2  66
      ## 2: 4.0.4 269
      ## 3: 4.0.3  17
dt[, .N, by = "OS_type"]  # all <NA>
      ##    OS_type   N
      ## 1:    <NA> 352
dt[, .N, by = .(NeedsCompilation)]   # 141 need!
      ##    NeedsCompilation   N
      ## 1:               no 185
      ## 2:              yes 160
      ## 3:             <NA>   7
# create subset, needs compilation
x  <- dt[NeedsCompilation == "yes"]
x[1:100]
      ##               Package OS_type NeedsCompilation Built
      ##   1:              ape    <NA>              yes 4.0.4
      ##   2:          askpass    <NA>              yes 4.0.2
      ##   3:               av    <NA>              yes 4.0.4

More advanced package managment:

List all packages + description, by library location

library()

(.packages())
      ##  [1] "microbenchmark" "data.table"     "forcats"        "stringr"       
      ##  [5] "readr"          "ggplot2"        "tidyverse"      "magrittr"      
      ##  [9] "dplyr"          "purrr"          "tidyr"          "tibble"        
      ## [13] "nvimcom"        "stats"          "graphics"       "grDevices"     
      ## [17] "utils"          "datasets"       "xfun"           "sloop"         
      ## [21] "here"           "lobstr"         "rlang"          "devtools"      
      ## [25] "usethis"        "reprex"         "methods"        "base"
(.packages(all.available  = TRUE))

Basic installation

  • sudo R CMD INSTALL *.tar.gz
  install.packages("igraph", verbose=TRUE, dependencies=TRUE)
      ## Installing package into '/home/jim/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.0'
      ## (as 'lib' is unspecified)
      ## system (cmd0): /usr/local/lib/R/bin/R CMD INSTALL
      ## Warning: dependency 'graph' is not available
      ## foundpkgs: igraph, /tmp/RtmpNPFi6c/downloaded_packages/igraph_1.2.6.tar.gz
      ## files: /tmp/RtmpNPFi6c/downloaded_packages/igraph_1.2.6.tar.gz
      ## 1): succeeded '/usr/local/lib/R/bin/R CMD INSTALL -l '/home/jim/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.0' '/tmp/RtmpNPFi6c/downloaded_packages/igraph_1.2.6.tar.gz''

workhorse

{
begin  <- Sys.time()
.libPaths()
update.packages(ask=FALSE )
end  <- Sys.time()
diff  <- end - begin
diff
}
      ## Time difference of 1.8 secs

Troubleshoot: install packages one at a time

{
begin  <- Sys.time()

# p  <- c("digest")
# p  <- c("future")
# p  <- c("httpuv")
# p  <- c("jsonlite")
# p  <- c("servr")
## p  <- c("lattice")
#p  <- c("utf8")
# p  <- c("rlang")

install.packages(p, verbose=TRUE, dependencies = TRUE)
# upgrade (p, ask = FALSE , checkBuild=TRUE)

end  <- Sys.time()
diff  <- end - begin
diff
}
      ## Error in install.packages(p, verbose = TRUE, dependencies = TRUE): object 'p' not found
# print updated summary table

Pkg Dependencies

pkg = "purrr"
packageDescription(pkg)
      ## Package: purrr
      ## Title: Functional Programming Tools
      ## Version: 0.3.4
      ## Authors@R: c(person(given = "Lionel", family = "Henry", role =
      ##        c("aut", "cre"), email = "lionel@rstudio.com"),
      ##        person(given = "Hadley", family = "Wickham", role = "aut",
      ##        email = "hadley@rstudio.com"), person(given = "RStudio",
      ##        role = c("cph", "fnd")))
      ## Description: A complete and consistent functional programming
      ##        toolkit for R.
      ## License: GPL-3 | file LICENSE
      ## URL: http://purrr.tidyverse.org,
      ##        https://github.com/tidyverse/purrr
      ## BugReports: https://github.com/tidyverse/purrr/issues
      ## Depends: R (>= 3.2)
      ## Imports: magrittr (>= 1.5), rlang (>= 0.3.1)
      ## Suggests: covr, crayon, dplyr (>= 0.7.8), knitr, rmarkdown,
      ##        testthat, tibble, tidyselect
      ## VignetteBuilder: knitr
      ## Encoding: UTF-8
      ## LazyData: true
      ## RoxygenNote: 7.1.0
      ## NeedsCompilation: yes
      ## Packaged: 2020-04-16 21:54:48 UTC; hadley
      ## Author: Lionel Henry [aut, cre], Hadley Wickham [aut], RStudio
      ##        [cph, fnd]
      ## Maintainer: Lionel Henry <lionel@rstudio.com>
      ## Repository: CRAN
      ## Date/Publication: 2020-04-17 12:10:07 UTC
      ## Built: R 4.0.4; x86_64-pc-linux-gnu; 2021-04-04 06:21:01 UTC;
      ##        unix
      ## 
      ## -- File: /home/jim/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.0/purrr/Meta/package.rds
# subset vector
packageDescription(pkg)[c("Imports", "Suggests")]
      ## $Imports
      ## [1] "magrittr (>= 1.5), rlang (>= 0.3.1)"
      ## 
      ## $Suggests
      ## [1] "covr, crayon, dplyr (>= 0.7.8), knitr, rmarkdown, testthat,\ntibble, tidyselect"

sessioninfo::package_info()

# For Installed Packages, yields dependencies
sessioninfo::package_info(pkg="tibble")

# ---- 004 R startup ----

# In this order, R seeks .Rprofile $R_HOME, $HOME, project directory
?Startup
R.home() # /usr/lib/R
      ## [1] "/usr/local/lib/R"
site_path = R.home(component = "home") 
site_path
      ## [1] "/usr/local/lib/R"
# file.path constructs path to a file
fname = file.path(site_path, "etc", "Rprofile.site") 
fname
      ## [1] "/usr/local/lib/R/etc/Rprofile.site"
file.exists(fname) 
      ## [1] FALSE

if pkg is installed, it will have DESCRIPTION file

# returns char[] , logical for each path
does_pkg_exist  <- function(path = .libPaths(), pkg = NULL,
                            file_name = "DESCRIPTION")

  file.exists(file.path(.libPaths(), pkg=pkg, "DESCRIPTION"))

pkg = "jimTools"
pkg = "foolish"
x  <- does_pkg_exist(pkg = pkg)
x
      ## [1] FALSE FALSE
